mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising inflation and falling industrial production indicate stagflationary supply shocks that compress profit margins and force monetary tightening, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when stagflation occurs, or SPX forward returns are uncorrelated with the combined index (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate revenue expectations and earnings per share, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in GDP acceleration periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals a sharp downturn in consumer confidence and construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts breach the 1.1 million threshold, showing no statistically significant drop (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightness, boosting near-term consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns during confirmed recoveries, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, further increases in the federal funds rate fail to anchor inflation expectations and instead trigger recession fears, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both rates and inflation are elevated, or the regime filter fails to isolate the predictive power (IC < 0.03 in the high-CPI subsample).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in retail sales and food services revenue indicates weakening aggregate demand and corporate margin pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral following the specified retail sales contraction, showing no statistically significant drop (p > 0.05).
